---
title: "<INSERT YOUR TITLE HERE>"
author: "<INSERT AUTHOR(S)>"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  docx:
    toc: false
    number-sections: true
bibliography: references.bib
csl: apa.csl
---

<!--
  Quarto manuscript scaffold for an SLR.

  The setup chunk below imports `build_stats()` from the sibling
  `analysis/stats.py`. Every number you cite in the prose must come
  from `s['some.key']` — never a literal in the Markdown. See the
  empirical-integrity skill for the full rationale and the test suite
  template for a regression check that greps for forbidden literals.

  Table functions live in `manuscript/_tables.py`. Keep prose readable
  by putting table logic there.

  Delete these HTML comments before final submission.
-->


```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# Make analysis/stats.py and manuscript/_tables.py importable
_ROOT = Path().resolve()
for _p in (_ROOT, _ROOT / "analysis", _ROOT / "manuscript"):
    if str(_p) not in sys.path:
        sys.path.insert(0, str(_p))

from stats import build_stats  # noqa: E402

s = build_stats()
```


# Introduction

<!-- Static prose — write by hand. No hand-typed methodology numbers. -->

This review addresses the research question:

> <INSERT YOUR RESEARCH QUESTION HERE>


# Methods

## Search strategy

We searched `{python} s['search.databases']` for articles published
between `{python} s['search.from_year']` and `{python} s['search.to_year']`,
filtered to `{python} s['search.journal_count']` journals selected for
their relevance to the research question (ABS Journal Guide 2024
rankings; full journal list in the supplementary `search_config.py`).

The combined search returned `{python} s['search.unique_records']`
unique records after deduplication by DOI and title+first-author
fallback for records without a DOI (`{python} s['search.unique_dois']`
records carry a DOI). The integrity-gatekeeper hash of the DOI set is
`{python} s['search.doi_sha256'][:12]`… (recorded in
`search_run.json`).

## Screening

### Stage 1 — abstract screening

Each record's title and abstract were screened by
`{python} s['provenance.abstract.model']` at temperature 0 with prompt
version `{python} s['provenance.abstract.prompt_version']`. Of the
`{python} s['screen.abstract.n_total']` records screened,
`{python} s['screen.abstract.n_include']` were marked **include**,
`{python} s['screen.abstract.n_borderline']` **borderline**, and
`{python} s['screen.abstract.n_exclude']` **exclude**. Borderline
records were retained for full-text review.

### Stage 2 — full-text screening and coding

The `{python} s['screen.fulltext.n_total']` records carried forward
were read in full by `{python} s['provenance.fulltext.model']` at
temperature 0 with prompt version
`{python} s['provenance.fulltext.prompt_version']`. The model
simultaneously rendered an include / exclude decision and, for every
include, extracted the coding fields listed in `screening_config.py`.
`{python} s['screen.fulltext.n_include']` papers were included for
final synthesis.

Every screening decision is in an append-only CSV log; the final
decision per paper is the last row keyed on `item_key`, which captures
any human-adjudication flips appended after the automated run.


# Findings

## Overview of the included corpus

```{python}
#| label: tbl-methods
#| tbl-cap: "Distribution of the included papers by research method."
#| echo: false
from _tables import tbl_methods
print(tbl_methods().to_markdown(index=False))
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-regions
#| tbl-cap: "Geographic regions represented in the sample."
#| echo: false
from _tables import tbl_sample_regions
print(tbl_sample_regions().to_markdown(index=False))
```

## <INSERT YOUR FIRST THEME / CONSTRUCT FAMILY / RESEARCH STREAM>

<!--
  For each theme, synthesise the findings across included papers.
  Do NOT walk one-paper-at-a-time — write thematic prose that cites
  multiple papers per claim. See the academic-writing skill's
  "SYNTHESIS over enumeration" rule.
-->


# Discussion

<!-- Static prose. Reference `s['key']` only for numbers. -->


# Limitations

The exclusion decisions are summarised in @tbl-exclusions. The
adjudication log (`screening/qa_review.md`) records every flip
between initial automated decision and final human judgment.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-exclusions
#| tbl-cap: "Full-text exclusions by reason code."
#| echo: false
from _tables import tbl_exclusion_reasons
print(tbl_exclusion_reasons().to_markdown(index=False))
```


# References

::: {#refs}
:::
